era_context: Same as previous years: Late Bronze Age collapse aftermath, early Iron Age consolidation. Egypt under Ramesses XI; Phoenician city-states rising; Aegean Geometric period; Mesopotamian weakness; Vedic period in South Asia.

events.title: Ramesses XI's reign continues; Egyptian political fragmentation ongoing
events.description: Ramesses XI continues his reign during a period of increasing fragmentation of Egyptian authority. By the mid-11th century BCE (within which -1025 falls), the division between the pharaoh's authority in Lower Egypt and the High Priests' control of Upper Egypt is well-established. This bifurcation of power is a defining feature of the late Ramessid period and represents a fundamental shift in Egyptian political structure that will persist into the Third Intermediate Period.

sources.name: Papyrus Harris I and administrative records

events.certainty_note: Ramesses XI's reign is well-attested, but specific events for -1025 are not documented.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed.
historiographic_note: Documentation for -1025 is sparse. We have no contemporary records of specific events for this year. Knowledge comes from administrative records spanning longer periods, archaeological stratigraphy, and later compilations. The record is heavily Egypt-centric and skewed toward elite perspectives.
